title: Fadogiella manikensis
intro: Fadogiella manikensis is a medicinal plant species native to West Africa, known for its traditional use in folk medicine. It has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Fadogiella manikensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Fadogiella manikensis is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Fadogiella manikensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Sri Lanka, and its classification places it within the genus Fadogiella. Further, it is a member of the family Rubiaceae, which consists of a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as coffee or madder family. The species Fadogiella manikensis was described by the botanist A. K. Gopalakrishnan in 1990. The classification of Fadogiella manikensis reflects its evolutionary relationships and characteristics within the plant kingdom.

Fadogiella manikensis is also valued for its role in traditional folk medicine in certain regions.
